title: How to Add a React Component
External Service Sync Spec
content:
# External Service Sync (el-6cq8)

## Context

Stoneforge tasks live locally. Teams that also use GitHub Issues or Linear need to manually duplicate work across systems. This feature adds bidirectional sync between Stoneforge elements and external services, starting with task↔GitHub Issues sync.

The architecture is designed for extensibility: documents↔Notion/Obsidian and messages↔Slack/Discord should plug in without restructuring. The abstraction has two layers — a **connection layer** (shared per provider: auth, webhooks, rate limits) and **sync adapters** (element-type-specific: tasks, documents, messages).

The codebase already has foundations: tasks have an `externalRef?: string` field (`packages/core/src/types/task.ts:241`), a mature JSONL sync system with content hashing and merge strategies, an event system that tracks all mutations, and background service patterns (dispatch daemon, auto-export).

**Automation**: Polling-based background sync. The daemon only starts when providers are configured — **zero overhead for unconfigured workspaces** (no daemon instantiation, no timers, no polling). Follows the same conditional-start pattern as the dispatch daemon.

---

## Architecture

### Two-Layer Provider Model

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Provider (e.g., GitHub)             │
│                                                   │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │  Connection Layer (shared)                  │ │
│  │  - Auth (token, OAuth)                      │ │
│  │  - API client (fetch-based, rate limits)    │ │
│  │  - Connection testing                        │ │
│  │  - Config (apiBaseUrl, project defaults)     │ │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                   │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌───────────┐  ┌───────────┐ │
│  │ TaskSync     │  │ DocSync   │  │ MsgSync   │ │
│  │ Adapter      │  │ Adapter   │  │ Adapter   │ │
│  │ (v1: GitHub) │  │ (future)  │  │ (future)  │ │
│  └──────────────┘  └───────────┘  └───────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

A **provider** handles connection concerns (auth, rate limits, webhooks). It bundles one or more **sync adapters**, each handling a specific element type. GitHub ships with a `TaskSyncAdapter`. A future Notion provider would ship with a `DocumentSyncAdapter`. Slack would ship with a `MessageSyncAdapter`.

### Package Placement

```
@stoneforge/core          → Provider + adapter interfaces, external item types, sync state types
@stoneforge/quarry        → Provider implementations, sync engine, field mapper, CLI commands
@stoneforge/smithy        → Background sync daemon, trigger endpoints, service wiring
```

### New Files

```
packages/core/src/types/external-sync.ts             # All interfaces + types

packages/quarry/src/external-sync/
  index.ts                                            # Public exports
  provider-registry.ts                                # Register/lookup providers
  sync-engine.ts                                      # Orchestrates adapters for push/pull/sync
  conflict-resolver.ts                                # Conflict detection + resolution strategies
  adapters/
    task-sync-adapter.ts                              # Shared task sync logic (field mapper, change detection)
  providers/github/
    index.ts                                          # Exports
    github-provider.ts                                # ExternalProvider impl (connection layer)
    github-api.ts                                     # GitHub REST API client (fetch-based)
    github-task-adapter.ts                            # TaskSyncAdapter impl for GitHub
    github-field-map.ts                               # GitHub-specific field mapping config

packages/quarry/src/cli/commands/external-sync.ts     # CLI commands

packages/smithy/src/services/external-sync-daemon.ts  # Background polling daemon
packages/smithy/src/server/routes/external-sync.ts    # Manual trigger + status endpoints
```

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/core/src/types/index.ts` | Export new types |
| `packages/core/src/index.ts` | Export new types |
| `packages/quarry/src/cli/runner.ts` | Register `externalSyncCommand` (~line 321) |
| `packages/quarry/src/config/types.ts` | Add `externalSync` config section |
| `packages/quarry/src/config/defaults.ts` | Add defaults |
| `packages/quarry/src/config/validation.ts` | Add validation |
| `packages/smithy/src/services/settings-service.ts` | Add `EXTERNAL_SYNC` to `SETTING_KEYS` + helpers |
| `packages/smithy/src/server/services.ts` | Add daemon to `Services` interface (~line 88) + init |
| `packages/smithy/src/server/index.ts` | Register sync routes, conditionally start daemon |
| `packages/smithy/src/server/routes/index.ts` | Export new routes |

---

## Key Interfaces

### Connection Layer

```typescript
/** A provider handles auth and API access for one external service. */
interface ExternalProvider {
  readonly name: string;              // e.g., 'github', 'linear', 'notion'
  readonly displayName: string;       // e.g., 'GitHub'

  testConnection(config: ProviderConfig): Promise<boolean>;

  /** Which adapter types this provider supports */
  readonly supportedAdapters: SyncAdapterType[];  // e.g., ['task'] for GitHub v1

  /** Get a specific adapter (returns undefined if not supported) */
  getTaskAdapter?(): TaskSyncAdapter;
  getDocumentAdapter?(): DocumentSyncAdapter;   // future
  getMessageAdapter?(): MessageSyncAdapter;     // future
}

/** Shared config for connecting to any provider */
interface ProviderConfig {
  provider: string;
  token?: string;
  apiBaseUrl?: string;
  defaultProject?: string;
}

type SyncAdapterType = 'task' | 'document' | 'message';
```

### Sync Adapters

Each adapter type has its own interface shaped for its element type:

```typescript
/** Task-specific sync adapter — maps between Stoneforge tasks and external issues */
interface TaskSyncAdapter {
  getIssue(project: string, externalId: string): Promise<ExternalTask | null>;
  listIssuesSince(project: string, since: Timestamp): Promise<ExternalTask[]>;
  createIssue(project: string, issue: ExternalTaskInput): Promise<ExternalTask>;
  updateIssue(project: string, externalId: string, updates: Partial<ExternalTaskInput>): Promise<ExternalTask>;
  getFieldMapConfig(): TaskFieldMapConfig;
}

/** Document-specific sync adapter (future — interface only for now) */
interface DocumentSyncAdapter {
  getPage(project: string, externalId: string): Promise<ExternalDocument | null>;
  listPagesSince(project: string, since: Timestamp): Promise<ExternalDocument[]>;
  createPage(project: string, page: ExternalDocumentInput): Promise<ExternalDocument>;
  updatePage(project: string, externalId: string, updates: Partial<ExternalDocumentInput>): Promise<ExternalDocument>;
}

/** Message-specific sync adapter (future — interface only for now) */
interface MessageSyncAdapter {
  listMessagesSince(channel: string, since: Timestamp): Promise<ExternalMessage[]>;
  sendMessage(channel: string, message: ExternalMessageInput): Promise<ExternalMessage>;
}
```

### External Item Types

```typescript
/** Normalized external task/issue — common shape across providers */
interface ExternalTask {
  externalId: string;
  url: string;
  provider: string;
  project: string;
  title: string;
  body?: string;
  state: 'open' | 'closed';
  labels: string[];
  assignees: string[];        // Read from external issues during pull
  /**
   * Native priority value, normalized to Stoneforge priority (1-5).
   * Set by providers that have native priority support (e.g., Linear).
   * Providers without native priority (e.g., GitHub) leave this undefined
   * and use label-based priority instead.
   */
  priority?: number;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
  closedAt?: string;
  raw?: Record<string, unknown>;  // Lossless round-tripping
}

/** Input for creating/updating an external task/issue */
interface ExternalTaskInput {
  title: string;
  body?: string;
  state?: 'open' | 'closed';
  labels?: string[];
  assignees?: string[];       // Ignored on push — assignees are pull-only
  /**
   * Native priority value as Stoneforge priority (1-5).
   * Providers with native priority support (e.g., Linear) convert this to
   * their native format (e.g., Linear 0-4). Providers without native priority
   * (e.g., GitHub) ignore this field and use label-based priority instead.
   */
  priority?: number;
}

/** Normalized external document (future) */
interface ExternalDocument {
  externalId: string;
  url: string;
  provider: string;
  project: string;
  title: string;
  content: string;
  contentType: 'markdown' | 'html' | 'text';
  updatedAt: string;
  raw?: Record<string, unknown>;
}

/** Normalized external message (future) */
interface ExternalMessage {
  externalId: string;
  url: string;
  provider: string;
  channel: string;
  sender: string;
  content: string;
  timestamp: string;
  raw?: Record<string, unknown>;
}
```

---

## Sync State Tracking

**Per-element**: Stored in `element.metadata._externalSync`:
- `provider`, `project`, `externalId`, `url`
- `lastPushedAt`, `lastPulledAt` — timestamps
- `lastPushedHash`, `lastPulledHash` — content hashes (reuses `computeContentHashSync()` from `packages/quarry/src/sync/hash.ts`)
- `direction` — push/pull/bidirectional
- `adapterType` — 'task' | 'document' | 'message'

**Per-provider (global)**: Stored in `SettingsService` (SQLite, not git-tracked — holds tokens):
- Provider configs (tokens, API URLs, default projects)
- Sync cursors per provider+project+adapterType (last poll timestamp)
- Poll interval, default direction, conflict strategy

**File-based config** (`ExternalSyncConfig` in `packages/quarry/src/config/types.ts`):
- `enabled` (boolean) — whether external sync is enabled
- `pollInterval` (Duration) — polling interval in ms
- `conflictStrategy` — last_write_wins, local_wins, remote_wins, manual
- `defaultDirection` — push, pull, bidirectional
- `autoLink` (boolean) — whether to auto-create external issues for new tasks
- `autoLinkProvider` (string, optional) — which provider to auto-link to (github or linear)

`element.externalRef` (on tasks — may need adding to other element types later) stores the canonical URL.

---

## Change Detection

- **Push**: Query events since `lastPushedAt` using `api.listEvents({ after, eventType: ['updated','closed','reopened'] })`, filter to elements with `_externalSync` metadata. Compare current content hash against `lastPushedHash`.
- **Pull**: Call adapter's `listXxxSince(since)` using the global sync cursor. Compare against `lastPulledHash`.

---

## Conflict Resolution

When both local and remote changed since last sync:
- **Default**: Last-write-wins (compare `updatedAt` timestamps)
- **Configurable**: `local_wins`, `remote_wins`, `manual`
- **Field-level merge**: Non-overlapping field changes merge automatically
- **Manual conflicts**: Tag element with `sync-conflict`, store both versions in metadata for CLI resolution

---

## Field Mapping (GitHub Tasks)

| Stoneforge | GitHub | Direction | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-----------|-------|
| `title` | `title` | Bidirectional | 1:1 |
| description (hydrated from `descriptionRef` doc) | `body` | Bidirectional | Hydrate doc for push; create doc on pull |
| `status` open/in_progress/review/blocked/deferred/backlog | `state: "open"` | Bidirectional | Many-to-one |
| `status` closed/tombstone | `state: "closed"` | Bidirectional | |
| `tags` | `labels` | Bidirectional | |
| `priority` 1-5 | labels `priority:critical` through `priority:minimal` | Bidirectional | Convention-based (GitHub has no native priority) |
| `taskType` | labels `type:bug`, `type:feature`, etc. | Bidirectional | Convention-based |
| `assignee` | `assignees` | Pull-only (read from GitHub, not written) | Read into task metadata on pull; ignored on push |

## Field Mapping (Linear Tasks)

| Stoneforge | Linear | Direction | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-----------|-------|
| `title` | `title` | Bidirectional | 1:1 |
| description (hydrated from `descriptionRef` doc) | `description` | Bidirectional | Markdown; hydrate doc for push, create doc on pull |
| `status` | `state.name` (workflow state) | Bidirectional | Mapped via workflow state lookup |
| `tags` | `labels[].name` | Bidirectional | |
| `priority` 1-5 | `priority` 0-4 | Bidirectional | Native priority mapping (see below) |
| `taskType` | labels `type:bug`, `type:feature`, etc. | Bidirectional | Convention-based |
| `assignee` | `assignee.displayName` | Pull-only (read from Linear, not written) | Read into task metadata on pull; ignored on push |

### Linear Priority Mapping

Linear has native priority support, so priority syncs directly via the `ExternalTask.priority` field rather than through labels. The mapping between Stoneforge priority (1-5) and Linear priority (0-4) is:

| Stoneforge | Linear | Meaning |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| 1 (critical) | 1 (Urgent) | Highest priority |
| 2 (high) | 2 (High) | |
| 3 (medium) | 3 (Medium) | |
| 4 (low) | 4 (Low) | |
| 5 (minimal) | 0 (No priority) | Lowest / unset |

When creating or updating Linear issues, Stoneforge priority is converted to Linear's native format. When pulling from Linear, the native priority is converted back to Stoneforge priority (1-5) and set on `ExternalTask.priority`. If no priority is provided on push, Linear defaults to 0 (No priority).

---

## Automation

**Polling daemon**: Background `setInterval` following dispatch daemon pattern (`packages/smithy/src/services/dispatch-daemon.ts`). Configurable interval (default 60s). Iterates all configured providers, runs each adapter's push + pull cycle.

**Zero-overhead guarantee**: The daemon is only instantiated and started when `externalSync.enabled === true` AND at least one provider has a configured token. Unconfigured workspaces pay no cost — no daemon object, no timers, no polling. Follows the same conditional-start pattern as the dispatch daemon in `packages/smithy/src/server/index.ts` (~lines 204-221).

---

## CLI Commands

```
sf external-sync config                              # Show provider config
sf external-sync config set-token <provider> <token>  # Store auth token
sf external-sync config set-project <provider> <repo> # Set default project
sf external-sync config set-auto-link <provider>      # Enable auto-link
sf external-sync config disable-auto-link             # Disable auto-link
sf external-sync link <taskId> <url-or-issue-number>  # Link task to external issue
sf external-sync unlink <taskId>                      # Remove link
sf external-sync push [taskId...]                     # Push linked task(s)
sf external-sync push --all                           # Push all linked tasks
sf external-sync pull                                 # Pull changes from external
sf external-sync sync [--dry-run]                     # Bidirectional sync
sf external-sync status                               # Show sync state
sf external-sync resolve <taskId> --keep local|remote # Resolve conflict
```

---

## Task Breakdown

### Phase 1: Foundation (Types + Registry)

1. **Define external sync types in `@stoneforge/core`** — `ExternalProvider`, `TaskSyncAdapter`, `DocumentSyncAdapter` (interface only), `MessageSyncAdapter` (interface only), `ExternalTask`, `ExternalDocument`, `ExternalMessage`, `ExternalSyncState`, `ProviderConfig`, `WebhookEvent`, `SyncAdapterType`, result/error types. Export from `types/index.ts` and `index.ts`. Workers should consult existing type patterns in `packages/core/src/types/task.ts` and `packages/core/src/types/element.ts`.

2. **Add config + settings support** — `ExternalSyncConfig` section in quarry config (`packages/quarry/src/config/types.ts`, `defaults.ts`, `validation.ts`). Add `EXTERNAL_SYNC` key to `SETTING_KEYS` in `packages/smithy/src/services/settings-service.ts` with `getExternalSyncSettings()`, `setExternalSyncSettings()`, `getProviderConfig()`, `setProviderConfig()` helpers.

3. **Create provider registry** — `packages/quarry/src/external-sync/provider-registry.ts`. `register(provider)`, `get(name)`, `list()`, `getAdaptersOfType(type)`. Ship with GitHub registered by default. Include `index.ts` exporting public API.

### Phase 2: Sync Engine

4. **Build sync engine** — `packages/quarry/src/external-sync/sync-engine.ts`. Core `push()`, `pull()`, `sync()` orchestration. Generic over adapter type — works with any adapter that returns `ExternalTask`/`ExternalDocument`/`ExternalMessage`. Event-based change detection for push (query `api.listEvents()`), cursor-based for pull. Content hash comparison via `computeContentHashSync()` from `packages/quarry/src/sync/hash.ts`.

5. **Build conflict resolver** — `packages/quarry/src/external-sync/conflict-resolver.ts`. Configurable strategies: `last_write_wins`, `local_wins`, `remote_wins`, `manual`. Field-level merge for non-overlapping changes. `sync-conflict` tag + metadata for manual resolution.

6. **Build task sync adapter utilities** — `packages/quarry/src/external-sync/adapters/task-sync-adapter.ts`. Shared field mapping logic: `taskToExternalTask()`, `externalTaskToTaskUpdates()`. Uses provider-supplied `TaskFieldMapConfig` for provider-specific mappings (status, priority labels, etc.). Handles description document hydration/creation.

### Phase 3: GitHub Provider

7. **GitHub API client** — `packages/quarry/src/external-sync/providers/github/github-api.ts`. Fetch-based, no external deps. Auth via PAT in headers. Rate limit handling (`X-RateLimit-*`). Configurable `apiBaseUrl` for GitHub Enterprise. Methods: `getIssue`, `listIssues(since)`, `createIssue`, `updateIssue`.

8. **GitHub provider + task adapter** — `packages/quarry/src/external-sync/providers/github/github-provider.ts` (implements `ExternalProvider`), `github-task-adapter.ts` (implements `TaskSyncAdapter`), `github-field-map.ts` (GitHub-specific `TaskFieldMapConfig`: priority labels, type labels, status mapping).

### Phase 4: CLI + Server

9. **CLI commands** — `packages/quarry/src/cli/commands/external-sync.ts`. All subcommands: `config`, `link`, `unlink`, `push`, `pull`, `sync`, `status`, `resolve`. Follow existing command pattern from `packages/quarry/src/cli/commands/sync.ts`. Register in `packages/quarry/src/cli/runner.ts`.

10. **Trigger + status endpoints** — `packages/smithy/src/server/routes/external-sync.ts`. `POST /api/external-sync/push`, `pull`, `sync` (manual triggers). `GET /api/external-sync/status`, `providers`. Register in `packages/smithy/src/server/routes/index.ts` and `packages/smithy/src/server/index.ts`.

11. **Background sync daemon + service wiring** — `packages/smithy/src/services/external-sync-daemon.ts`. Follow dispatch daemon pattern: `start()`/`stop()`/`isRunning()` with `setInterval`. Add to `Services` interface in `packages/smithy/src/server/services.ts` (~line 88). Initialize in `initializeServices()`. **Only instantiate and start when `externalSync.enabled` AND at least one provider configured** — otherwise set to `undefined` in Services (same pattern as `dispatchDaemon` and `poolService`).

### Phase 5: Tests

12. **Tests** — Unit tests for: types/validation, provider registry, field mapper round-trips, sync engine (mock adapter), conflict resolver, GitHub API client (mock HTTP), CLI commands.

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests**: `bun test packages/quarry/src/external-sync/` and `bun test packages/core/src/types/external-sync`
2. **Manual CLI test**:
   - `sf external-sync config set-token github <PAT>`
   - `sf external-sync config set-project github owner/repo`
   - `sf task create --title "Test sync"` → get task ID
   - `sf external-sync push <taskId>` → verify issue created on GitHub
   - Edit issue title on GitHub
   - `sf external-sync pull` → verify task title updated locally
   - `sf external-sync status` → verify sync state
3. **Background sync test**:
   - Enable daemon, create/edit linked issues, verify sync happens within poll interval
4. **Zero-overhead test**: Start server with no providers configured, verify no external sync daemon in logs
5. **Typecheck**: `bun run typecheck` from project root

title: CLI Reference: Data & Communication Commands
content:
content            # CLI Reference: Data & Communication Commands

Commands for managing entities, documents, plans, workflows, channels, messages, sync, and more.

## Entity Commands

| Command                                    | Description          |
| ------------------------------------------ | -------------------- |
| `sf entity register <name>`                | Register new entity  |
| `sf entity list`                           | List entities        |
| `sf entity set-manager <entity> <manager>` | Set manager          |
| `sf entity clear-manager <entity>`         | Clear manager        |
| `sf entity reports <manager>`              | Get direct reports   |
| `sf entity chain <entity>`                 | Get management chain |

#### entity register

Register a new entity.

| Option                    | Description                                         |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `-t, --type <type>`       | Entity type: agent, human, or system (default: agent) |
| `--public-key <key>`      | Base64-encoded Ed25519 public key                   |
| `--tag <tag>`             | Add tag (can be repeated)                           |

```bash
sf entity register alice --type human
sf entity register bot-1 --type agent --tag worker
```

#### entity list

List registered entities.

| Option                | Description                       |
| --------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-t, --type <type>`   | Filter by entity type             |
| `-l, --limit <n>`     | Maximum results                   |

```bash
sf entity list --type agent
```

## Document Commands

| Command                               | Description                                   |
| ------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `sf document create`                  | Create document                               |
| `sf document list`                    | List documents                                |
| `sf document search <query>`          | Full-text search documents                    |
| `sf document show <id>`               | Show document                                 |
| `sf document update <id>`             | Update document content (creates new version) |
| `sf document history <id>`            | Show version history                          |
| `sf document rollback <id> <version>` | Rollback to version                           |
| `sf document archive <id>`            | Archive document                              |
| `sf document unarchive <id>`          | Unarchive document                            |
| `sf document delete <id>`             | Delete document (soft-delete via tombstone)    |
| `sf document reindex`                 | Reindex documents for FTS5 search             |

#### document create

Create a new document.

| Option                  | Description                                    |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `--title <text>`        | Document title                                 |
| `-c, --content <text>`  | Document content (inline)                      |
| `-f, --file <path>`     | Read content from file                         |
| `-t, --type <type>`     | Document type: text, markdown, or json         |
| `--category <cat>`      | Document category                              |
| `--tag <tag>`           | Add tag (can be repeated)                      |
| `-m, --metadata <json>` | JSON metadata                                  |

#### document list

List documents.

| Option                   | Description                                           |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `-l, --limit <n>`        | Maximum results                                       |
| `-t, --type <type>`      | Filter by document type                               |
| `--category <cat>`       | Filter by category                                    |
| `--status <status>`      | Filter by status: active or archived                  |
| `-a, --all`              | Include archived documents                            |

#### document show

Show document content.

| Option                    | Description                       |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `--doc-version <ver>`     | Show specific version             |

#### document search

Full-text search documents.

| Option                   | Description                       |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------- |
| `--category <cat>`       | Filter by category                |
| `--status <status>`      | Filter by status                  |
| `-l, --limit <n>`        | Maximum results (default: 50)     |

#### document history

Show version history for a document.

| Option              | Description                       |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-l, --limit <n>`   | Maximum results                   |

```bash
sf document create --title "API Spec" --category spec
sf document list --category spec
sf document search "API authentication"
sf document update el-doc123 --file updated-spec.md
sf document archive abc123
sf document reindex
```

#### document update

Update a document's content, creating a new version. Documents are versioned - each update preserves history.

| Option                    | Description                       |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-c, --content <text>`    | New content (inline)              |
| `-f, --file <path>`       | Read new content from file        |
| `-m, --metadata <json>`   | JSON metadata to merge            |

```bash
sf document update el-doc123 --file path/to/updated.md
```

#### document delete

Delete a document via soft-delete (tombstone). The document's content is preserved but marked as deleted.

| Option                  | Description              |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `-r, --reason <text>`   | Reason for deletion      |
| `-f, --force`           | Skip confirmation        |

```bash
sf document delete el-doc123 --reason "outdated"
```

## Embeddings Commands

| Command                        | Description                    |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------ |
| `sf embeddings install`        | Install local embedding model  |
| `sf embeddings status`         | Show embedding model status    |
| `sf embeddings reindex`        | Rebuild document embeddings    |
| `sf embeddings search <query>` | Semantic search over documents |

```bash
sf embeddings install
sf embeddings search "authentication flow"
```

## Plan Commands

| Command                           | Description                       |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `sf plan create`                  | Create plan (defaults to draft)   |
| `sf plan list`                    | List plans                        |
| `sf plan show <id>`               | Show plan details                 |
| `sf plan activate <id>`           | Activate plan (enables dispatch)  |
| `sf plan complete <id>`           | Mark completed                    |
| `sf plan cancel <id>`             | Cancel plan                       |
| `sf plan add-task <id> <task>`    | Add task to plan                  |
| `sf plan remove-task <id> <task>` | Remove task                       |
| `sf plan tasks <id>`              | List tasks in plan                |
| `sf plan auto-complete`           | Auto-complete active plans        |

#### plan create

Create a new plan.

| Option                  | Description                                         |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `-t, --title <text>`    | Plan title (required)                               |
| `-s, --status <status>` | Initial status: draft (default) or active           |
| `--tag <tag>`           | Add tag (can be repeated)                           |

```bash
sf plan create --title "Feature X" --tag roadmap
```

#### plan list

List plans.

| Option                  | Description                                         |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `-s, --status <status>` | Filter by status: draft, active, completed, cancelled |
| `--tag <tag>`           | Filter by tag (can be repeated for AND logic)       |
| `-l, --limit <n>`       | Maximum results                                     |

```bash
sf plan list --status active
```

#### plan show

Show plan details, optionally including its task list.

| Option           | Description              |
| ---------------- | ------------------------ |
| `-t, --tasks`    | Include task list        |

```bash
sf plan show el-abc123 --tasks
```

#### plan auto-complete

| Option      | Description                                           |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `--dry-run` | Show what would be auto-completed without making changes |

```bash
sf plan auto-complete --dry-run
```

#### plan cancel

Cancel a plan.

| Option                | Description              |
| --------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `-r, --reason <text>` | Reason for cancellation  |

```bash
sf plan cancel el-plan123 --reason "Superseded"
```

#### plan tasks

List tasks belonging to a plan.

| Option                  | Description                       |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-s, --status <status>` | Filter by task status             |
| `-l, --limit <n>`       | Maximum results                   |

```bash
sf plan tasks el-plan123 --status open
```

### Draft Plan Workflow

Plans default to `draft` status. Tasks in draft plans are NOT dispatchable. Activate a plan after setting up tasks and dependencies to enable dispatch.

## Workflow Commands

| Command                         | Description               |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------- |
| `sf workflow create <playbook>` | Instantiate from playbook |
| `sf workflow list`              | List workflows            |
| `sf workflow show <id>`         | Show details              |
| `sf workflow tasks <id>`        | List tasks                |
| `sf workflow progress <id>`     | Show progress             |
| `sf workflow delete <id>`       | Delete ephemeral          |
| `sf workflow promote <id>`      | Promote to durable        |
| `sf workflow gc`                | Garbage collect           |

#### workflow list

List workflows.

| Option                  | Description                              |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `-s, --status <status>` | Filter by status                         |
| `-e, --ephemeral`       | Show only ephemeral workflows            |
| `-d, --durable`         | Show only durable workflows              |
| `-l, --limit <n>`       | Maximum results                          |

```bash
sf workflow list --status running --ephemeral
```

#### workflow tasks

List tasks in a workflow.

| Option                  | Description                       |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-r, --ready`           | Show only ready tasks             |
| `-s, --status <status>` | Filter by task status             |
| `-l, --limit <n>`       | Maximum results                   |

```bash
sf workflow tasks el-wf123 --ready
```

#### workflow delete

Delete a workflow.

| Option          | Description                                   |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `-f, --force`   | Required for deleting durable workflows       |

```bash
sf workflow delete el-wf123 --force
```

#### workflow create

| Option                  | Description                               |
| ----------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `--var <name=value>`    | Set variable (can be repeated)            |
| `-e, --ephemeral`       | Create as ephemeral (not synced to JSONL) |
| `-t, --title <text>`    | Override workflow title                   |

```bash
sf workflow create deploy --var env=prod --ephemeral
```

#### workflow gc

| Option              | Description                                      |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `-a, --age <days>`  | Maximum age in days (default: 7)                 |
| `--dry-run`         | Show what would be deleted without deleting      |

```bash
sf workflow gc --age 14
```

## GC Commands

| Command           | Description                                    |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `sf gc tasks`     | Garbage collect old tasks (deprecated, no-op)  |
| `sf gc workflows` | Garbage collect old ephemeral workflows         |

### sf gc workflows

```bash
sf gc workflows [options]
```

| Option              | Description                              |
| ------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `-a, --age <days>`  | Maximum age in days (default: 1)         |
| `--dry-run`         | Show what would be deleted               |
| `-l, --limit <n>`   | Maximum number of workflows to delete    |

```bash
sf gc workflows --age 7 --dry-run
```

### sf gc tasks (deprecated)

Task garbage collection is now handled automatically via `sf gc workflows`. This command is kept for backwards compatibility but is effectively a no-op.

## Inbox Commands

| Command                 | Description                          |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `sf inbox <agent-id>`   | List inbox items with message preview |
| `sf inbox read <id>`    | Mark as read                         |
| `sf inbox read-all <entity>` | Mark all as read for entity     |
| `sf inbox unread <id>`  | Mark as unread                       |
| `sf inbox archive <id>` | Archive item                         |
| `sf inbox count <entity>` | Count unread for entity            |
| `sf show <inbox-id>`    | Show inbox item with full content    |

#### inbox list

List inbox items with message content preview.

| Option                   | Description                                          |
| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `-a, --all`              | Include read and archived items (default: unread only) |
| `-s, --status <status>`  | Filter by status: unread, read, or archived          |
| `-l, --limit <n>`        | Maximum number of items to return                    |
| `-F, --full`             | Show complete message content instead of truncated   |

```bash
sf inbox alice --full
sf show inbox-abc123
```

## Channel Commands

| Command                           | Description                                             |
| --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `sf channel create`               | Create channel (`--description, -D` to set description) |
| `sf channel list`                 | List channels                                           |
| `sf channel join <id>`            | Join channel                                            |
| `sf channel leave <id>`           | Leave channel                                           |
| `sf channel members <id>`         | List members                                            |
| `sf channel add <ch> <entity>`    | Add member                                              |
| `sf channel remove <ch> <entity>` | Remove member                                           |
| `sf channel merge`                | Merge two channels                                      |

#### channel create

| Option                     | Description                                      |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `-n, --name <name>`        | Channel name (required for group channels)       |
| `-D, --description <text>` | Channel description                              |
| `-t, --type <type>`        | Channel type: group (default) or direct          |
| `--visibility <vis>`      | Visibility: public or private (default)          |
| `-p, --policy <policy>`    | Join policy: open, invite-only (default), or request |
| `-m, --member <id>`        | Add member (can be repeated)                     |
| `-d, --direct <entity>`    | Create direct channel with entity (for --type direct) |
| `--tag <tag>`              | Add tag (can be repeated)                        |

```bash
sf channel create --name general --description "General discussion"
sf channel create --type direct --direct el-user123
```

#### channel list

List channels.

| Option                | Description                              |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `-t, --type <type>`   | Filter by type: group or direct          |
| `-m, --member <id>`   | Filter by member                         |
| `-l, --limit <n>`     | Maximum results                          |

```bash
sf channel list --type group --member alice
```

#### channel merge

| Option                  | Description                              |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `-s, --source <id>`     | Source channel ID (required)             |
| `-t, --target <id>`     | Target channel ID (required)             |
| `-n, --name <new-name>` | Optional new name for the merged channel |

```bash
sf channel merge -s el-ch111 -t el-ch222 --name "combined-channel"
```

## Message Commands

| Command                  | Description                                        |
| ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `sf message send`        | Send message to channel, entity, or as reply       |
| `sf message reply <id>`  | Reply to a message (shorthand for send --reply-to) |
| `sf message thread <id>` | View thread messages                               |
| `sf message list`        | List messages                                      |

#### message send

| Option                  | Description                                                                      |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-c, --channel <id>`    | Channel to send to                                                               |
| `-T, --to <entity>`     | Entity to send DM to (finds or creates DM channel)                               |
| `-r, --reply-to <msg>`  | Message ID to reply to (auto-sets channel, thread, swaps sender/recipient in DM) |
| `-m, --content <text>`  | Message content                                                                  |
| `--file <path>`         | Read content from file                                                           |
| `-t, --thread <id>`     | Reply to message (creates thread)                                                |
| `-a, --attachment <id>` | Attach document (can be repeated)                                                |
| `--tag <tag>`           | Add tag (can be repeated)                                                        |

```bash
sf message send --channel el-abc123 --content "Hello!"
sf message send --to el-user456 -m "Direct message"
sf message send --reply-to el-msg789 -m "Reply"
```

#### message reply

| Option                  | Description                       |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-m, --content <text>`  | Message content                   |
| `--file <path>`         | Read content from file            |
| `-a, --attachment <id>` | Attach document (can be repeated) |
| `--tag <tag>`           | Add tag (can be repeated)         |

```bash
sf message reply el-msg123 --content "Thanks for the update!"
sf --from bot message reply el-msg123 -m "Automated response"
```

#### message list

| Option                 | Description                              |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `-c, --channel <id>`   | Channel ID (required)                    |
| `-s, --sender <id>`    | Filter by sender                         |
| `-r, --root-only`      | Show only root messages (no replies)     |
| `-l, --limit <n>`      | Maximum results                          |

```bash
sf message list --channel el-ch123 --sender alice
```

#### message thread

View thread messages for a message.

| Option              | Description                       |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-l, --limit <n>`   | Maximum results                   |

```bash
sf message thread el-msg123
sf message thread el-msg123 --limit 50
```

## Team Commands

| Command                          | Description   |

| Option                | Description                       |

List teams.

| Option                | Description                       |
| --------------------- | --------------------------------- |

| Option                | Description              |
| --------------------- | ------------------------ |

| `-f, --force`         | Skip confirmation for teams with members |


#### library create

Create a new library.

| Option                | Description                       |

| `-n, --name <name>`   | Library name (required)           |

#### library list

List libraries.

| Option              | Description                       |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-l, --limit <n>`   | Maximum results                   |

#### library delete

Delete a library. By default, deletion is prevented if the library has contents.

| Option              | Description                                         |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `-f, --force`       | Force deletion even if library has contents         |

```bash

sf library delete el-lib123 --force
```

## Doctor Command

Check system health and diagnose issues.

```bash
sf doctor [options]
```

| Option       | Description                                           |
| ------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `--fix`      | Automatically repair detected issues (FK violations, blocked cache) |

**Checks performed:**

Database health:
- Workspace exists (`.stoneforge` directory)
- Database file exists and can be opened
- Schema version is current
- All expected tables are present
- Database integrity check (`PRAGMA integrity_check`)
- Foreign key constraint validation
- Blocked cache consistency
- Storage statistics

Runtime health (via smithy-server, skipped if unavailable):
- Rate limit status: which executables are limited, when they reset
- Stuck tasks: tasks with high resumeCount and no active session
- Merge queue health: tasks stuck in testing/merging
- Error rate: recent errors from operation log
- Agent pool: utilization and active sessions

**Auto-repair (`--fix`):**
- Deletes orphaned rows that violate foreign key constraints
- Rebuilds the blocked cache from the dependency graph

```bash
sf doctor
sf doctor --verbose
sf doctor --fix
sf doctor --json
```

## Migrate Command

Run database migrations to update the schema.

```bash
sf migrate [options]
```

| Option       | Description                                           |
| ------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `--dry-run`  | Show what migrations would be applied without running them |

Migrations are run automatically when needed, but this command can be used to manually trigger migration or check pending migrations.

```bash
sf migrate
sf migrate --dry-run
```

## Sync Commands

| Command           | Description                       |
| ----------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `sf sync export`  | Export elements to JSONL files    |
| `sf sync import`  | Import elements from JSONL files  |
| `sf sync status`  | Show sync status                  |

Top-level commands `sf export`, `sf import`, and `sf status` are also available as shortcuts.

#### sync export

Export elements and dependencies to JSONL files for version control. By default, only exports elements modified since the last export (incremental).

```bash
sf sync export [options]
```

| Option                  | Description                                   |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `-o, --output <dir>`    | Output directory (default: `.stoneforge/sync`) |
| `-f, --full`            | Full export (ignore dirty tracking)           |
| `--include-ephemeral`   | Include ephemeral elements (excluded by default) |

```bash
sf export
sf export --full
sf export -o ./backup
```

#### sync import

Import elements and dependencies from JSONL files. Uses Last-Write-Wins (LWW) merge strategy by default.

```bash
sf sync import [options]
```

| Option                | Description                                    |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `-i, --input <dir>`   | Input directory (default: `.stoneforge/sync`)  |
| `-n, --dry-run`       | Show what would change without importing       |
| `-f, --force`         | Force import (remote always wins)              |

```bash
sf import
sf import --dry-run
sf import --force
```

#### sync status

Show the current sync status (dirty element count, sync directory status).

```bash
sf sync status
sf status
```

## External Sync Commands

Manage bidirectional sync with external services (GitHub, Linear, folder-based providers).

| Command                              | Description                                          |
| ------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `sf external-sync config`            | Show provider configuration                          |
| `sf external-sync config set-token`  | Set authentication token for a provider              |
| `sf external-sync config set-project`| Set default project for a provider                   |
| `sf external-sync config set-auto-link` | Enable auto-linking for a provider                |
| `sf external-sync config disable-auto-link` | Disable auto-linking                           |
| `sf external-sync link`              | Link a task or document to an external issue/page    |
| `sf external-sync link-all`          | Bulk-link all unlinked tasks or documents            |
| `sf external-sync unlink`            | Remove external link from a task or document         |
| `sf external-sync unlink-all`        | Bulk-remove external links from all linked elements  |
| `sf external-sync push`              | Push linked elements to external service             |
| `sf external-sync pull`              | Pull changes from external for linked elements       |
| `sf external-sync sync`              | Bidirectional sync (push + pull)                     |
| `sf external-sync status`            | Show external sync state overview                    |
| `sf external-sync resolve`           | Resolve a sync conflict                              |

#### external-sync config

Show current external sync configuration including enabled providers, conflict strategy, and auto-link settings.

```bash
sf external-sync config
```

#### external-sync config set-token

```bash
sf external-sync config set-token <provider> <token>
```

```bash
sf external-sync config set-token github ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxx
sf external-sync config set-token linear lin_api_xxxxxxxxxxxx
```

#### external-sync config set-project

```bash
sf external-sync config set-project <provider> <project>
```

```bash
sf external-sync config set-project github owner/repo
sf external-sync config set-project linear PROJECT-KEY
```

#### external-sync config set-auto-link

Enable auto-linking new elements to the specified provider.

```bash
sf external-sync config set-auto-link <provider> [options]
```

| Option                | Description                                          |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `-t, --type <type>`   | Element type: `task` or `document` (default: `task`) |

```bash
sf external-sync config set-auto-link github
sf external-sync config set-auto-link folder --type document
```

#### external-sync config disable-auto-link

```bash
sf external-sync config disable-auto-link [options]
```

| Option                | Description                                                    |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-t, --type <type>`   | Element type: `task`, `document`, or `all` (default: `all`)    |

#### external-sync link

Link a task or document to an external issue or page.

```bash
sf external-sync link <elementId> <url-or-external-id> [options]
```

| Option                  | Description                                    |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `-p, --provider <name>` | Provider name (default: `github`)              |
| `-t, --type <type>`     | Element type: `task` or `document` (default: `task`) |

```bash
sf external-sync link el-abc123 https://github.com/org/repo/issues/42
sf external-sync link el-doc456 https://linear.app/team/DOC-1 --provider linear --type document
```

#### external-sync link-all

Bulk-link all unlinked tasks or documents to a provider. Creates external issues/pages for each unlinked element.

```bash
sf external-sync link-all [options]
```

| Option                    | Description                                                      |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-p, --provider <name>`   | Provider to link to (required)                                  |
| `--project <project>`     | Override the default project                                    |
| `-s, --status <status>`   | Only link elements with this status (repeatable)                |
| `-n, --dry-run`            | List elements that would be linked without creating issues      |
| `-b, --batch-size <n>`    | Elements to process concurrently (default: 10)                  |
| `-f, --force`              | Re-link elements already linked (including same provider)       |
| `-t, --type <type>`       | Element type: `task` or `document` (default: `task`)            |
| `--no-library`             | Include documents not in any library (excluded by default)      |

```bash
sf external-sync link-all --provider github --dry-run
sf external-sync link-all --provider github --status open --status in_progress
sf external-sync link-all --provider folder --type document
```

#### external-sync unlink

Remove the external link from a task or document.

```bash
sf external-sync unlink <elementId>
```

#### external-sync unlink-all

Bulk-remove external links from all linked elements.

```bash
sf external-sync unlink-all [options]
```

| Option                  | Description                                               |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-p, --provider <name>` | Only unlink elements linked to this provider             |
| `-t, --type <type>`     | Element type: `task`, `document`, or `all` (default: `all`) |
| `-n, --dry-run`          | Show what would be unlinked without making changes       |

```bash
sf external-sync unlink-all --provider github --dry-run
sf external-sync unlink-all --type task
```

#### external-sync push

Push linked elements to their external service. Only pushes elements that have changed since the last sync.

```bash
sf external-sync push [elementId...] [options]
```

| Option                | Description                                                    |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-a, --all`           | Push all linked elements                                       |
| `-f, --force`         | Push all regardless of changes                                 |
| `-t, --type <type>`   | Element type: `task`, `document`, or `all` (default: `all`)    |
| `--no-library`         | Include documents not in any library (excluded by default)    |

```bash
sf external-sync push el-abc123
sf external-sync push --all
sf external-sync push --all --force --type task
```

#### external-sync pull

Pull changes from external services for linked elements.

```bash
sf external-sync pull [options]
```

| Option                  | Description                                               |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-p, --provider <name>` | Provider to pull from (default: all configured)          |
| `-d, --discover`        | Discover new external issues not yet linked              |
| `-t, --type <type>`     | Element type: `task`, `document`, or `all` (default: `all`) |

```bash
sf external-sync pull
sf external-sync pull --provider github --discover
sf external-sync pull --type document
```

#### external-sync sync

Bidirectional sync — pushes local changes and pulls remote changes.

```bash
sf external-sync sync [options]
```

| Option                | Description                                                    |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-n, --dry-run`       | Show what would change without making changes                  |
| `-t, --type <type>`   | Element type: `task`, `document`, or `all` (default: `all`)    |

```bash
sf external-sync sync
sf external-sync sync --dry-run
sf external-sync sync --type task
```

#### external-sync status

Show external sync state overview including linked elements and their sync status.

```bash
sf external-sync status
```

#### external-sync resolve

Resolve a sync conflict for a linked element.

```bash
sf external-sync resolve <elementId> [options]
```

| Option                | Description                                    |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `-k, --keep <version>`| Which version to keep: `local` or `remote` (required) |

```bash
sf external-sync resolve el-abc123 --keep local
sf external-sync resolve el-abc123 --keep remote
```

## Docs Commands

Documentation infrastructure commands.

| Command          | Description                                    |
| ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `sf docs init`   | Bootstrap Documentation library and directory  |
| `sf docs add`    | Add document(s) to the Documentation library   |
| `sf docs dir`    | Show the Documentation Directory document      |

#### docs init

Idempotently bootstrap the Documentation library and Documentation Directory document. Safe to run multiple times.

```bash
sf docs init
```

#### docs add

Add one or more documents to the Documentation library (must exist first via `sf docs init`).

```bash
sf docs add <doc-id> [doc-id2 ...]
```

```bash
sf docs add el-doc123
sf docs add el-doc123 el-doc456 el-doc789
```

#### docs dir

Show the Documentation Directory document.

| Option       | Description                              |
| ------------ | ---------------------------------------- |
| `--content`  | Include the full document content        |

```bash
sf docs dir
sf docs dir --content
```

## Playbook Commands

Manage playbooks (workflow templates).

| Command                            | Description                              |
| ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `sf playbook list`                 | List playbooks                           |
| `sf playbook show <name\|id>`      | Show playbook details                    |
| `sf playbook validate <name\|id>`  | Validate playbook structure              |
| `sf playbook create`               | Create a new playbook                    |

#### playbook list

| Option              | Description                       |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-l, --limit <n>`   | Maximum results                   |

```bash
sf playbook list
```

#### playbook show

| Option              | Description                       |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `-s, --steps`       | Include step definitions          |
| `--variables`       | Include variable definitions      |

```bash
sf playbook show deploy
sf playbook show deploy --steps --variables
```

#### playbook validate

Validate a playbook's structure and optionally test create-time variable resolution.

| Option                  | Description                                           |
| ----------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `--var <name=value>`    | Set variable for create-time validation (repeatable)  |
| `-c, --create`          | Perform create-time validation                        |

```bash
sf playbook validate deploy
sf playbook validate deploy --create
sf playbook validate deploy --var env=production --var debug=true
```

#### playbook create

| Option                  | Description                                         |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `-n, --name <name>`     | Playbook name (unique identifier, required)         |
| `-t, --title <title>`   | Playbook title (display name, required)             |
| `-s, --step <spec>`     | Add step: `id:title[:dep1,dep2]` (repeatable)       |
| `--variable <spec>`     | Add variable: `name:type[:default][:required]` (repeatable) |
| `-e, --extends <name>`  | Extend playbook (repeatable)                        |
| `--tag <tag>`           | Add tag (repeatable)                                |

```bash
sf playbook create --name deploy --title "Deployment Process"
sf playbook create -n deploy -t "Deploy" -s "build:Build app" -s "test:Run tests:build"
```
